Cost (USD): Go to aws.amazon.com > Create Account > Enter email, password, credit card
AWS Console > IAM > Root user > Security credentials > Assign MFA > Use Google Authenticator app
IAM > Users > Create user > Name: brickpro-admin > Attach policy: AdministratorAccess > Enable console access
IAM > Users > brickpro-admin > Security credentials > Create access key > Download CSV
Download from aws.amazon.com/cli > Install > Open terminal > Run: aws configure > Enter Access Key ID, Secret, Region: ap-south-1, Output: json
AWS Console > Billing > Budgets > Create budget > $10 monthly budget > Email alert at 80%
VPC Console > Create VPC > Name: brickpro-vpc > IPv4 CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 > Create
VPC > Subnets > Create > Name: brickpro-public-1a > VPC: brickpro-vpc > AZ: ap-south-1a > CIDR: 10.0.1.0/24
VPC > Subnets > Create > Name: brickpro-private-1a > VPC: brickpro-vpc > AZ: ap-south-1a > CIDR: 10.0.2.0/24
VPC > Internet Gateways > Create > Name: brickpro-igw > Attach to brickpro-vpc
VPC > Route Tables > Create > Name: brickpro-public-rt > Add route: 0.0.0.0/0 → brickpro-igw > Associate with public subnet
VPC > Security Groups > Create > Name: brickpro-ec2-sg > Inbound: SSH(22) from My IP, HTTP(80) from 0.0.0.0/0, HTTPS(443) from 0.0.0.0/0, Custom(4000) from 0.0.0.0/0
VPC > Security Groups > Create > Name: brickpro-rds-sg > Inbound: PostgreSQL(5432) from brickpro-ec2-sg only
RDS > Subnet groups > Create > Name: brickpro-db-subnet > VPC: brickpro-vpc > Add both subnets (need 2 AZs, create another private subnet in 1b if needed)

RDS > Create database > PostgreSQL 15 > Free tier template > DB instance: brickpro-db > Master username: postgres > Password: <strong-password> > db.t3.micro > 20GB > VPC: brickpro-vpc > Subnet group: brickpro-db-subnet > Security group: brickpro-rds-sg > No public access
EC2 > Key Pairs > Create > Name: brickpro-key > Type: RSA > Format: .pem > Download and save securely

EC2 > Launch Instance > Name: brickpro-api-server > AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 > Type: t3.micro > Key pair: brickpro-key > VPC: brickpro-vpc > Subnet: brickpro-public-1a > Auto-assign public IP: Enable > Security group: brickpro-ec2-sg > Storage: 20GB gp3
EC2 > Elastic IPs > Allocate > Associate with brickpro-api-server (so IP doesn't change on restart)

Create file: nano .env → Add: DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:<password>@<rds-endpoint>:5432/brickpro  JWT_SECRET=<random-64-chars>  PORT=4000  NODE_ENV=production
npx prisma migrate deploy && npx prisma generate
npm run build && pm2 start dist/index.js --name brickpro-api && pm2 save && pm2 startup

sudo nano /etc/nginx/conf.d/brickpro.conf → Add: server { listen 80; server_name api.brickpro.in; location / { proxy_pass http://localhost:4000; proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade; } }
sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
sudo certbot --nginx -d api.brickpro.in (domain must already point to EC2 IP)
Route 53 > Hosted zones > Create > Domain: brickpro.in > Note the 4 NS records
Go to domain registrar > DNS settings > Replace nameservers with Route 53 NS records

ACM > Request certificate > Domain: *.brickpro.in and brickpro.in > DNS validation > Create CNAME records in Route 53
S3 > Create bucket > Name: brickpro-web-prod > Region: ap-south-1 > Uncheck 'Block all public access' > Create
S3 > brickpro-web-prod > Properties > Static website hosting > Enable > Index: index.html > Error: index.html
Local: cd apps/web && npm run build (creates dist/ folder)

CloudFront > Create distribution > Origin: brickpro-web-prod.s3.amazonaws.com > Viewer protocol: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS > Alternate domain: brickpro.in > SSL: ACM cert > Default root: index.html > Custom error: 403→/index.html (200)
Route 53 > Create record > Name: (empty for root) > Type: A > Alias: Yes > CloudFront distribution
Same steps 40-46 for admin panel with bucket: brickpro-admin-prod and domain: admin.brickpro.in
Download APK from expo.dev dashboard → Install on Android phone → Test all features
Play Console > Create app > Upload AAB > Fill store listing > Submit for review

RDS > Modify > Backup retention: 7 days > Apply

Section: Tell me about a project you deployed on AWS
Item: I built BrickPro, a multi-tenant SaaS for brick factories. Deployed on EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, S3 for static hosting, CloudFront CDN, Route 53 DNS, and ACM for SSL. Handles production tracking, sales, and financial reporting for multiple factories.

Used VPC with public/private subnets, RDS in private subnet accessible only from EC2 security group, IAM roles for S3 access, JWT authentication, bcrypt password hashing, Helmet for HTTP headers, and rate limiting.

Designed multi-tenant architecture with client isolation. Can scale EC2 vertically or move to ECS Fargate for horizontal scaling. Database can be upgraded to Multi-AZ RDS. Static assets served via CloudFront CDN.

Designed and managed PostgreSQL on RDS with 20+ tables, complex relationships, Prisma ORM for migrations. Wrote aggregation queries for reports (monthly P&L, customer analytics, production summaries).
Built reporting system that aggregates production, sales, and expense data. Can extend with AWS Glue ETL to move data to Redshift for advanced analytics and QuickSight dashboards.
